world.title: Trump says ballroom is needed after correspondents’ dinner shooting

world.description: In June 2023, the Japanese Parliament (Diet) passed a controversial amendment to the Immigration Control Act, establishing stricter regulations for asylum seekers and increasing enforcement for deportations. world.title: Starmer faces vote on possible probe over Mandelson

world.description: "I can't imagine that there's any profession that is more dangerous," Donald Trump said of his job, just hours after he was at the centre of yet another major security incident.

science.title: Rare ribosome tweak in E. coli reveals possible antibiotic target
science.description: Storing genetic material as DNA or RNA is all well and good for life on Earth, but it would be entirely pointless if we couldn't do anything with it. To use our genetic blueprints, all organisms need to translate the message from their nucleic acid forms into protein, which then become the building blocks that make up each organism. Because this is such a critical part of life, ribosomes—the machinery responsible for the translation of RNA into protein—are fairly conserved across all living things.

This 1,100-Foot Asteroid Will Pass Just 20,000 Miles from Earth and Scientists Call It “Very Rare”
science.description: A giant asteroid is approaching, and researchers say this kind of encounter almost never happens. What they expect to observe could reveal something entirely new about how these objects behave in space.

ai.title: J&J sees AI halving the time to generate drug development leads
ai.description: Johnson & Johnson is leveraging artificial intelligence to significantly speed up drug development. The company has halved the time needed to identify promising new drug candidates. AI is also streamlining manufacturing processes and drastically reducing the time for preparing regulatory documents.
